items.title: Schema completeness returns exactly the missing-field set
items.precondition: Fixture page carrying a LocalBusiness/Dentist entity with only @type/name/address/telephone
steps: 1. Score the fixture with scoreSchemaCompleteness
2. Compare the returned missingFields with the expected set
3. Confirm every graded field is a real schema.org field
items.expected: missingFields equals areaServed, geo, image, openingHours, priceRange, sameAs, url; no invented field ever appears; a non-business @type reports no gradable entity
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-checker.test.ts > 'scoreSchemaCompleteness — missing-field set' — 3/3 passed in the 31-test focused run and the 487-test full suite.
items.notes: Grading is new logic layered over the reused extractIdentityFromHtml parse (reconcile-first rule).

items.title: Honesty gate blocks rank/placement/visibility copy without false positives
items.precondition: GeneratedCopySafetyService.validateGeneratedCopy run over the rejected and honest copy sets
steps: 1. Submit 14 ranking/placement/visibility promises
2. Submit 6 honest copy lines, including negated disclaimers
items.expected: Every promise returns isSafe false / status blocked with reason codes; every honest line returns isSafe true / status safe
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-checker.test.ts > 'honesty lint — GeneratedCopySafetyService.validateGeneratedCopy' — 20/20 passed (14 rejected, 6 honest) inside the 751-test full suite on the round-6 head.
items.notes: Extends the existing bounded-regex scanner; the negation guard keeps 'does not guarantee a higher ranking' passing. Value #6 — no ranking promises. The service is the neutral shared one under src/services/content-safety/ (moved out of the GBP domain in round 4 per §7.1).

items.title: AEO-incomplete gates nothing and never reaches owner-facing copy
items.precondition: Checker run on an AEO-incomplete fixture page with honest candidate copy
steps: 1. Assert the internal signal is present
2. Assert recommendations are still emitted and the honesty gate still passes
3. Scan every recommendation title/detail for the signal
items.expected: internalSignals contains AEO-incomplete, recommendations are non-empty, honesty passes, and no owner-facing string matches /AEO/i
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-checker.test.ts > 'AEO-incomplete gates nothing and is never owner-facing' — 3/3 passed in the 31-test focused run.
items.notes: The signal is admin-only and blocks nothing.

items.title: GBP<->page consistency flag is read-only and skips cleanly without a GBP
items.precondition: Checker run with, and without, a supplied GBP identity
steps: 1. Run with gbpIdentity omitted
2. Run with a GBP identity that disagrees with the page
items.expected: Omitted -> gbpPageConsistency is null (flag skipped); disagreeing -> a non-null flag is returned; nothing is written either way
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-checker.test.ts — the skip and divergence cases passed in the 31-test focused run.
items.notes: Reuses compareExternalIdentity. Read-only: no DB insert/update, no GBP write.

items.title: Answer-first lint enforces the boundary in document order (review fix)
items.precondition: Fixture pages placing a 32-word substantive answer at different document positions
steps: 1. Answer before the first form
2. Answer only AFTER the first form
3. Answer buried past the element cap
4. Answer only in a later top-level section
5. Answer only after a CTA
6. A nav CTA ahead of an early answer
items.expected: Only the answer-before-form and nav-chrome pages omit answer_not_first; the other four flag it, each reporting the boundary that stopped the scan (form, element_cap, next_section, cta)
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-checker.test.ts > 'answer-first lint — document-order boundary' — 6/6 passed. Proven a real regression: all 4 flagging cases returned [] against the pre-fix code (expected [] to include 'answer_not_first').
items.notes:
Root cause: the old code computed firstFormIndex, stored it in details, and never applied it — it scanned the first 6

elements wherever they sat. Replaced with a document-order walk halting at the first form/CTA/section boundary.

items.title: The checker has a production call site in the real per-page audit path
items.precondition: executeTargets invoked with the audit pipeline's collaborators (models, collector, scoring, persistence) mocked
steps: 1. Execute one page target and assert the checker was invoked
2. Execute a target whose fetch failed
3. Make the checker throw and assert the audit still scores and persists
items.expected: The checker runs once per successfully fetched page, reusing the audit's own snapshot html/finalUrl (no second request); it is skipped for unfetched pages; a checker throw never fails the audit run
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-production-wiring.test.ts — 3/3 passed. Proven a real lock: removing the runGetFoundAdvisory(entry.snapshot) call makes it fail with 'expected vi.fn() to be called 1 times, but got 0 times'.
items.notes: LIMITATION — unit-level with the audit pipeline mocked, NOT behavioral validation against a running app; the running-app check is T7. Before this fix runGetFoundChecker had no caller outside the test suite.

items.title: Observability hook emits on a real queued audit run against a live hosted page
items.precondition: A running app: Postgres, Redis/BullMQ, the ai-seo-audit worker, and a real hosted practice page
steps: 1. Queue an organization or url_only audit run against a live hosted page
2. Let the ai-seo-audit worker execute the run
3. Grep the worker log for the '[get-found] checker run complete' line
4. Confirm the run's score and persisted results are unchanged by the advisory checker
items.expected: One structured log line per fetched page carrying checker/url/schemaMissingFields/answerFirstFlags/honestyChecked/internalSignals, and no change to the audit's score or persisted rows
items.evidence: Not auto-run by the agent. No verification was performed against a running app.
items.notes: The one item genuinely requiring Layer-2 behavioral validation. The wiring is verified structurally (T6) and by tsc, but the log has not been observed firing from the real worker.
items.waiver: Not executable in the review environment: no Postgres/Redis/BullMQ and no live hosted practice page, and a claude/* branch does not deploy to dev. Dave closes this by running one audit on dev after merge and confirming '[get-found] checker run complete' appears once per fetched page. Risk is bounded: the call is guarded by try/catch, persists nothing, and cannot alter the audit score (T6).

items.title: Repository gates are green with no regression
items.precondition: Node 22.23.1 in a clean worktree of claude/slice-1a-get-found
steps: 1. npx tsc --noEmit
2. npm run check:conventions -- --strict
3. npm test (full suite)
items.expected: tsc exit 0; 0 clear structural violations; the full suite shows no new failures against the pre-change baseline
items.evidence: Round 6, exit codes read directly from each command (never through a pipe). npx tsc --noEmit: exit 0. npm run check:conventions -- --strict: exit 0, 'CLEAR structural violations — backend (size + console + db): 0'. npx vitest run (full suite): exit 0, 63 files / 751 tests, 0 failures. npm run lint: exit 0 (warn-level advisory baseline, §18). npm run depcruise: exit 0 (38 warnings, advisory baseline, §18). Baseline on the reviewed head c56797e9 was 62 files / 637 tests / exit 0; this round adds +114 (honesty/normalization regressions + the new copy-normalization unit).
items.notes: check:all is NOT used as the receipt: it chains its steps with ';', so only the last command's exit code survives and a mid-chain failure reads as green. Each gate was run separately and its own exit code recorded. Round 6 also fixed a §2.4 violation it had itself introduced — GeneratedCopySafetyService.ts reached 859 lines, past the ~800 ceiling — by extracting the negation model to claimNegation.ts (317 + 560 lines).

items.title: Current GitHub checks attached to the PR
items.precondition: A CI workflow triggering on the claude/slice-1a-get-found branch
steps: 1. Read the PR's check runs
2. Attach the results to the acceptance artifacts
items.expected: Green CI check runs are attached to PR #158
items.evidence: Not possible. No CI is configured for claude/* branches — the workflows trigger on dev/dave (dev.yml) and main (main.yml) only, so PR #158 has no check runs to attach.
items.notes: Raised with Dave separately; not a defect in this PR.
items.waiver: No CI exists for this branch pattern, so there are no checks to attach. The equivalent evidence is T8: the same gates run locally on Node 22 with their exact output recorded.

items.title: The two round-5 bypass classes are closed, and the fix is proven non-vacuous
steps: 1. Measure every named bypass against the REVIEWED head (c56797e9) before changing anything
2. Apply the encoding fold + the governing-negator rules
3. Re-measure; then revert the fix with the fixtures kept and confirm they fail
items.expected: Every measured bypass blocks after the fix; no honest-copy fixture regresses; the new fixtures fail against the pre-fix service
items.evidence:
MEASURED ON THE REVIEWED HEAD FIRST: all 5 of Dave's cases passed (isSafe=true) plus 10/13 encoding probes — 'no boundary' (。 ；
) and 'negator that negates nothing' (no-hassle, without delay) confirmed live. AFTER: 0/14 and 0/13 defects. NON-VACUOUS: with the two source files reverted to c56797e9 and the fixtures kept, vitest exits 1 with 42 failures; restoring gives exit 0 / 751 passed.
items.notes: Both classes are fixed as CLASSES, not as the named strings: the boundary fix is a normalization pass (Unicode Sentence_Terminal property — not an enumerated list of terminators — plus NFKC, Cf-stripping, entity decoding, confusable folding, HTML break/inline tag handling) applied BEFORE matching; the negator fix demotes a negator that governs nothing (hyphen-compound / fixed idiom / juxtaposed NP fragment) instead of treating any token as a clause flag. Residuals are NAMED in claimNegation.ts and copyNormalization.ts, not papered over. This remains a conservative FILTER — no exhaustiveness is claimed anywhere.

items.title: Round 6 found and fixed 11 defects in its OWN fix before shipping
items.precondition: The round-6 fix, attacked in both directions by the author and then by an independent adversary against the already-fixed version
steps: 1. Attack the fix for over-blocks (honest copy wrongly blocked) and under-blocks
2. Have an independent adversary attack the FIXED version
3. Fix every confirmed defect and pin it as a regression fixture
items.expected: No self-found or adversary-found defect ships unfixed or unrecorded
items.evidence:
11 defects, all fixed and pinned; 5 were OVER-BLOCKS — the silent failure direction. SELF-ATTACK (7): demoting 'no other'/'no less'/'no fewer' as idioms wrongly BLOCKED honest disclaimers ('We make no other guarantees about your google ranking') — those entries were wrong, removed or narrowed to the comparative-THAN forms; four under-blocks in the forward negation path and idiom inventory ('Top placement is no problem', 'has never been easier'). Also self-found: an O(n^2) this round introduced in its own negator walk — 375ms on an 18KB many-negator input, now 2.1ms. INDEPENDENT ADVERSARY against the fixed version (4 more): (a) OVER-BLOCK — '
' folded to a PARAGRAPH break (a hard clause end) blocked 'We do not
guarantee a higher ranking'; '
' renders as ONE line break and is the HTML soft wrap, so it now folds to a single '\n', which still ends scope before a new subject so Dave's boast form still blocks. One change satisfies both. (b) OVER-BLOCK — 'not only' negates exclusivity and genuinely scopes over what follows, so demoting it blocked 'Good dentistry is not only about your google rankings'; entry removed. (c) UNDER-BLOCK — the 'no problem' demotion fired only clause-finally, so 'is no problem for us' (the MORE natural boast) laundered; it now accepts a trailing PP or the clause end but never a finite clause, so 'is not a problem we can solve' still passes. (d) UNDER-BLOCK — confusable folding covered only Cyrillic/Greek; Latin Extended-IPA, Armenian and Cherokee look-alikes now fold too.
items.notes: Recorded because the idiom inventory is the part of this gate most likely to be widened again, and widening it carelessly fails SILENTLY in the direction nothing downstream catches — 5 of 11 defects this round were exactly that. Every over-block is pinned as a must-pass fixture in get-found-checker.test.ts. The 'not only' reversal is a deliberate accepted MISS ('We not only guarantee top placement' now passes) taken to avoid the over-block, and is named as a residual rather than hidden.
